name: STEIN-LEVENTHAL SYNDROME
definition: POLYCYSTIC OVARY SYNDROME functional NOT imaging diagnosis

demographics.Prevalence: 2.5-8% of women in reproductive age; affects 4-5 million women in USA
demographics.Etiology: deficient aromatase activity (= catalyst for conversion of androgen into estrogen) →
demographics.Path: pearly white ovaries with multiple cysts below the capsule → lined by a hyperplastic
demographics.Associated with: Cushing syndrome, basophilic pituitary adenoma, postpill amenorrhea,

dominant_finding: normal ovarian size (in 30%), polycystic ovaries have a volume of 6-30 cm3
discriminating_features: at least one enlarged ovary > 10 cm3
normal ovarian size (in 30%), polycystic ovaries have a volume of 6-30 cm3
multiple (> 12) follicles of 2-9 mm in subcapsular location

general: at least one enlarged ovary > 10 cm3
normal ovarian size (in 30%), polycystic ovaries have a volume of 6-30 cm3
multiple (> 12) follicles of 2-9 mm in subcapsular location

clinical.Cx: endometrial cancer < 40 years of age ← unopposed chronic estrogen stimulation
